entries.text: Codex built REST endpoints in a project that wasn't using REST — had no context about the project's architecture.
Don't use Claude Code for tasks where Andy already has full context — just use edit/write tools directly, or dispatch to Buzz/Woody.
Never display openclaw.json raw — it contains secrets (API keys, bot tokens, gateway auth). Always filter with jq or redact.
Don't edit openclaw.json with jq pipe + mv — it can corrupt/revert the config and crash the gateway. Use openclaw config commands.
Buzz missed chatModeRef in voice-chat app — same stale closure pattern he'd already fixed once. Recurring blind spot.
qwen3:14b spent 103s thinking then dumped fake JSON in content instead of using tool API. Local models unreliable for multi-step tool-calling.
Synthetic tool-calling benchmarks don't predict real-world performance. Complex cron with tools → use Claude Sonnet or bash scripts.
Always give full project context in prompts — stack, architecture, patterns, what NOT to do. Prevents agents from guessing wrong.
For coding/build tasks, default to using the squad workflow (Buzz/Woody + Sarge review) rather than ad-hoc one-off Codex runs.
Buzz ships fast and clean when given good context. Stale closures are his recurring blind spot — call it out in prompts.
Never push directly to main. Always feature branch (andy/<description>), test, then merge.
When we make changes to any app that has a Tauri version, always port changes to the Tauri version too.
Simple single-tool tasks → qwen3:8b/14b OK. Text generation only → any model. Complex multi-tool → cloud models only.
Bash scripts > AI for mechanical checks (disk, backup, etc). More reliable and cheaper.
Full voice pipeline working: mic → local Whisper STT → OpenAI-compatible LLM → MiniMax TTS → speaker. Push-to-talk with spacebar.
Packages tab tracks Fedora rawhide vs stable versions with color-coded status (current/outdated/missing/beta).
Credential proxy running — agents get scoped short-lived JWT tokens; vault proxies API calls with real secrets server-side.
Multiple WebSocketServer instances on one HTTP server with path option causes 400 Bad Request. Use noServer: true with manual upgrade routing instead.
Vite proxy only handles HTTP by default. WebSocket paths need explicit proxy config with ws: true — otherwise WS connections hit the Vite server and fail.
Restarting the backend server kills Vite dev server proxy connections, causing ECONNREFUSED errors. Vite doesn't auto-recover — needs manual restart.
sql.js exec() doesn't support parameterized queries. Use prepare() + bind() + step() + getAsObject() for safe parameterized access.
Tauri frontend components need API_BASE defaulting to http://localhost:3851, not empty string. Web version uses Vite proxy so empty works, Tauri has no proxy.

Andy is 'The Conductor' — conductors don't play instruments, they conduct. ALWAYS delegate coding work to assigned agents (Buzz, Woody, etc). Andy's job: oversee, dispatch, review, and be available for Tux. Create new agents (Toy Story themed) if needed, but never do the coding yourself.
Learning Log tab — accordion categories, filter pills (errors/learnings/features), add entry form, server CRUD to data/learning-log.json.
System dashboard tab — real-time CPU/memory/disk/GPU monitoring via WebSocket, 32-core heatmap, canvas history charts, top processes, podman containers, SQLite metrics storage.
Coinbase SDK returns typed objects (not dicts) — .get() fails but getattr works. Always test against real API responses, not just dict assumptions.
Paper trading must enforce the same risk checks as live trading, otherwise paper results are misleading and dangerous. Always inject RiskManager into both engines.
Stop-loss logic blocked ALL orders including sells — should only block buys. A stop-loss should trigger a sell, not prevent one.
Smoke-testing API connectivity before code review catches bugs reviewers miss (reading code vs running code). Do both.
Coinbase SDK returns typed objects, not dicts. Always check hasattr + getattr patterns alongside dict access. SDK candle granularity uses string enums (FIVE_MINUTE) not ints (300).
Tests using date.today() are timezone-dependent — will fail when local date != UTC date (every evening PST). Always use datetime.now(timezone.utc).date() in tests that interact with UTC-based code.
Config field name mismatches (macd_signal vs macd_signal_period) break **vars() unpacking. When using dataclass splat, verify field names match exactly between config and target class.
Background exec sessions have timeouts — engines/servers die when the session expires. For persistent services, use systemd or detach properly from the session lifecycle.

entries.title: Discord blocks Python urllib default User-Agent
entries.description: Discord webhooks return 403 Forbidden when called with Python's default `Python-urllib/3.x` User-Agent. curl works fine because it sends a normal UA. Fix: set a custom User-Agent header on webhook requests.
entries.text: Discord blocks Python urllib default User-Agent — Discord webhooks return 403 Forbidden when called with Python's default `Python-urllib/3.x` User-Agent. curl works fine because it sends a normal UA. Fix: set a custom User-Agent header on webhook requests.

entries.title: SQLite row_factory race condition in FastAPI thread pool
entries.description: SQLite connection with check_same_thread=False shared across FastAPI's sync endpoint thread pool can lose row_factory setting, causing IndexError when accessing rows as dicts. Fix: add threading.Lock and re-set row_factory before every query.
entries.text: SQLite row_factory race condition in FastAPI thread pool — SQLite connection with check_same_thread=False shared across FastAPI's sync endpoint thread pool can lose row_factory setting, causing IndexError when accessing rows as dicts. Fix: add threading.Lock and re-set row_factory before every query.

entries.title: Serve frontend static files from FastAPI (single port)
entries.description: Instead of running a separate Vite dev server in production, build the frontend and serve via FastAPI's StaticFiles mount. Single port for API + UI. Frontend BASE_PATH switches between '/api' (dev proxy) and '' (production, same origin).
entries.text: Serve frontend static files from FastAPI (single port) — Instead of running a separate Vite dev server in production, build the frontend and serve via FastAPI's StaticFiles mount. Single port for API + UI. Frontend BASE_PATH switches between '/api' (dev proxy) and '' (production, same origin).

entries.title: Env var override gotcha: empty string vs unset
entries.description: os.getenv('VAR', 'fallback') returns '' (not fallback) when VAR is set but empty in .env. This silently overrode a valid TOML config value for the Discord webhook URL. Lesson: use `os.getenv('VAR') or fallback` pattern, or don't set empty env vars.
entries.text: Env var override gotcha: empty string vs unset — os.getenv('VAR', 'fallback') returns '' (not fallback) when VAR is set but empty in .env. This silently overrode a valid TOML config value for the Discord webhook URL. Lesson: use `os.getenv('VAR') or fallback` pattern, or don't set empty env vars.

entries.title: Cron delivery needs explicit channel with multi-channel configs
entries.description: OpenClaw cron jobs with delivery.mode='announce' fail when multiple channels (telegram, discord) are configured without specifying delivery.channel. Fix: set channel explicitly in the delivery config.
entries.text: Cron delivery needs explicit channel with multi-channel configs — OpenClaw cron jobs with delivery.mode='announce' fail when multiple channels (telegram, discord) are configured without specifying delivery.channel. Fix: set channel explicitly in the delivery config.

entries.title: Position sizing must match asset prices
entries.description: max_position_usd of $100-$3000 is meaningless for BTC where ATR-based sizing creates ~$8K+ notional positions. Orchestrator signals were silently risk-blocked for weeks. Always validate risk limits against actual position sizes at current asset prices.
entries.text: Position sizing must match asset prices — max_position_usd of $100-$3000 is meaningless for BTC where ATR-based sizing creates ~$8K+ notional positions. Orchestrator signals were silently risk-blocked for weeks. Always validate risk limits against actual position sizes at current asset prices.

entries.title: Backtest bugs can hide strategy performance
entries.description: Backtest engine never forwarded allow_short to PaperTradingEngine — short signals silently blocked. Combined with undersized max_position, orchestrator showed 0 trades in backtests, making it look broken when it was actually generating good signals.
entries.text: Backtest bugs can hide strategy performance — Backtest engine never forwarded allow_short to PaperTradingEngine — short signals silently blocked. Combined with undersized max_position, orchestrator showed 0 trades in backtests, making it look broken when it was actually generating good signals.

entries.title: Tight risk per trade + proper sizing = best risk-adjusted returns
entries.description: Backtesting 12 months of BTC/ETH data: 0.5% risk per trade with proper position sizing (+55%, 0.98 Sharpe on BTC) beat aggressive configs. Tighter stops (1.0 ATR vs 1.5) and higher ADX thresholds (25 vs 20) improved quality of trades.
entries.text: Tight risk per trade + proper sizing = best risk-adjusted returns — Backtesting 12 months of BTC/ETH data: 0.5% risk per trade with proper position sizing (+55%, 0.98 Sharpe on BTC) beat aggressive configs. Tighter stops (1.0 ATR vs 1.5) and higher ADX thresholds (25 vs 20) improved quality of trades.

entries.title: Cap indicator window for backtest speedup
entries.description: Backtest engine was passing full candle history to indicators on every tick. Capping to last 500 candles gave 23x speedup (10min to 26sec for 12-month orchestrator run) with identical results.
entries.text: Cap indicator window for backtest speedup — Backtest engine was passing full candle history to indicators on every tick. Capping to last 500 candles gave 23x speedup (10min to 26sec for 12-month orchestrator run) with identical results.

Dynamic agent status tracking: use a shared status.json file that the orchestrator writes to on dispatch/completion, with process detection as a fallback (30min timeout). Trusting the file over process checks avoids false negatives during startup.
Follow dispatch protocol: dont hand-code features yourself when squad agents should do the work. Conductor conducts, agents build. Even for partial/WIP work, commit to branch and hand off.
cargo build --release does NOT embed frontend assets in Tauri. Only cargo tauri build embeds dist/ into the binary. Without it, the webview shows Connection Refused (falls back to devUrl).
Ref-wrap WebSocket handlers to prevent stale closures. Store in useRef, update .current each render, call handlerRef.current() from WS listener. Sarge review caught this pattern.
The Conductor rule works: dispatch to squad, let them build, Sarge reviews, Andy applies fixes and deploys. Caught a real bug (missing busy guard) that would have shipped without review.
